title: advance_diversifier_to doc lies: no row created for idx=0
description: Scenario: doc says 'Creates the account row if it does not exist (upsert)' but the implementation guards with 'if idx <= current { return Ok(()) }' where current defaults to 0 for a missing row. advance_diversifier_to(account, 0) reads current=0 via unwrap_or(0), then 0 <= 0 is true, returns Ok() without creating any row. The doc promise is broken for idx=0. Maintenance consequence: a future caller who reads the doc and skips ensure_account before calling advance_diversifier_to(account, 0) will silently not create the row; the subsequent next_diversifier call will bail with 'call ensure_account() first'.
